lenses.guidance: Providence is the chapter's heart. The servant prays (vv 12-14), the answer arrives before he finishes (v 15), and he marvels: 'the LORD has led me' (v 27). The redemptive line continues by divine arrangement, not engineering — the chapter sermons quiet trust in God's ordering.

The servant's prayer (vv 12-14) is a model. He asks specifically, ties his request to God's covenant kindness (chesed), and acts in faith while waiting for the answer. When it comes, he worships immediately (v 26) — devotion that bookends petition with praise.

The bride-sought-for-the-son pattern echoes through Scripture: Moses meets Zipporah at a well (Ex 2:15-21), Jacob meets Rachel at a well (Gen 29:1-12), Christ meets the Samaritan woman at a well (John 4) — the bridegroom imagery culminates at Revelation 19:7.
